title: Image Processing Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of image loading, preprocessing, normalization, and augmentation for computer vision.

questions.question: OpenCV's cv2.imread() loads images in which channel order?

options.text: RGB — Red, Green, Blue
BGR — Blue, Green, Red
HSV — Hue, Saturation, Value
Grayscale — single channel

questions.question: Why is image normalization (scaling pixels from [0,255] to [0,1] or using mean/std) important before training a neural network?

options.text: It compresses the image file to save disk space
It improves gradient flow during backpropagation by keeping input values in a stable range, leading to faster and more stable training
It makes the images smaller so they fit in GPU memory
It converts the image to grayscale automatically

questions.question: Why is data augmentation applied during training but typically NOT during inference?

options.text: Augmentation increases image file size, which slows down serving
Augmentation randomly transforms images to increase training data diversity and robustness; during inference you want deterministic, consistent predictions
Augmentation can only run on CPUs, which are not available during inference
Augmentation requires the ground truth label to apply the correct transform

questions.question: What is the ImageNet-standard input size that most pretrained models (ResNet, VGG, EfficientNet) expect?

options.text: 512 × 512 pixels
128 × 128 pixels
224 × 224 pixels
1024 × 1024 pixels

questions.question: Which interpolation method should you use when SHRINKING an image with cv2.resize()?

options.text: cv2.INTER_CUBIC — best for all cases
cv2.INTER_NEAREST — fastest but lowest quality
cv2.INTER_AREA — recommended for downscaling as it reduces aliasing artifacts
cv2.INTER_LINEAR — only works for grayscale images
